gazette: General Directorate of Trade Research and Risk Assessment
title: Amendment to the Resolution on the Promotion of Foreign Direct Investments in Serbia
bodyHtml:
The amendment to the investment incentives legislation has been approved by the Government of the Republic of Serbia in order to attract foreign direct investment. Under the previous practice, companies investing a minimum of 100,000 euros in underdeveloped regions could benefit from incentives. However, with the legislative change, the minimum investment amount has been increased to 300,000 euros. Additionally, the additional support provided to investments of strategic importance has been eliminated, and privileges are now granted exclusively for green projects and technologies.

According to the Decision Amending the Decision on Determining the Criteria for Granting Incentives for Attracting Direct Investments ("Official Gazette of RS", No. 39/2023) adopted in May 2023, the criteria and elements of incentives necessary to benefit from the incentives will be determined based on the regional system used in local administration (previously determined based on the level of development).

Click here for a detailed announcement on the subject.
